title: indulgent fresh slovakian shiso-leaf lamination-puff
description: A delicate Slovakian pastry featuring shiso leaf and lamination puff, Ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly, and perfect for a light dessert or snack.

ingredients.name: lamination puff pastry
ingredients.notes: homemade or store-bought, thawed according to package instructions

instructions: Roll out the lamination puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
In a small bowl, mix together the almond milk, honey, and vanilla extract (if using).
Brush the mixture evenly over the pastry, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Arrange the fresh shiso leaves on top of the pastry, slightly overlapping each other to form a pattern.
Sprinkle the granulated sugar over the shiso leaves, if using.
Fold the edges of the pastry up over the shiso leaves to form a crust.
Brush the coconut oil over the crust to give it a golden brown color.
If using fresh mint, sprinkle it evenly over the pastry.
Transfer the pastry to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the pastry in the preheated oven for 18-20 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the pastry is cooked through.
Remove the pastry from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Slice the pastry into 12 equal pieces and serve warm, dusting with powdered sugar if desired.
